events.title: NYC Summer Kendama Cup 2026
events.description:
NYC Summer Kendama Cup 2026 🏆
Join us for the NYC Summer Kendama Cup 2026, New York City’s premier Kendama event celebrating competition, creativity, and community.
Watch top Kendama players compete in Freestyle and Speed Ladder tournaments, enjoy beginner-friendly activities, and experience one of Japan’s most iconic skill toys in the heart of Brooklyn.
Whether you’re a longtime player or trying Kendama for the first time, everyone is welcome. Come connect with the community, cheer on the competitors, and discover why Kendama is growing around the world.
📅 Saturday, July 11, 2026
🕛 12:00 PM – 7:00 PM
📍 Japan Village, Industry City, Brooklyn
Free admission for spectators.
Presented by Japanklyn.
events.vendor: NYC Summer Kendama Cup 2026

events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

events.title: Dried Flower Resin Craft Workshop: Hosted by little bloomtale × KAJIHA
events.description:
Join our Resin Craft Accessary Workshop by little bloomtale hosted by Kajiha!
Discover the art of resin crafting and make your own beautiful jewelry pieces filled with our dried flowers!
In this hands-on, relaxing workshop, you’ll design and create your very own resin accessories—now with even more options to choose from, including necklaces, earrings, bracelets, and keychains (with more to come!).
• All materials provided
• Perfect for beginners and craft lovers alike
• Take home your own handmade accessory
Let your creativity bloom and craft something truly special with us!
events.vendor: Dried Flower Resin Craft Workshop: Hosted by little bloomtale × KAJIHA

events.title: Introduction to Sake and Brewery Tour
events.description:
If you’ve wanted to learn the basics of sake in a fun and approachable way, this Introduction to Sake tasting class is for you! Join us in person at the Sake Studies Center at Brooklyn Kura in NYC to learn the basics of sake and enjoy a guided tasting of the most delicious sakes. We invite you to taste and learn about sake where sake is made! Get a guided brewery tour, taste and explore a set of 5 premium sakes including some fresh from the press! Let’s level up your sake knowledge and have fun doing it!
What’s included:
A welcome sake to get you started!
Sake 101 presentation to cover all the basics
Guided Tasting of 4 Brooklyn Kura craft sakes as well as a premium sake from Japan.
Handouts so you can bring your sake knowledge home
Tasting some sake fresh from the press!
Guided tour through our new Sake Brewery
Some nibbles to pair with your sakes
GIVING THIS CLASS AS A GIFT??
You can send an e-gift card for our “Introduction to Sake” class.
To purchase an electronic gift card, Please visit:
https://www.sakestudiescenter.com/giftcard
events.vendor: Introduction to Sake and Brewery Tour

events.title: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
events.description:
Join us for a tour of our state-of-the-art sake brewery in Industry City, Brooklyn. See first hand how we turn rice and water into delicious Brooklyn-made premium craft sake. Led by our expert brewery staff, this experience will walk you through our production facility and allow you to take in the sights, sounds and delicious smells of New York’s premier sake brewery. The tour concludes in our taproom where we will lead you a guided tasting flight of 3 kinds of Brooklyn Kura and introduce you to the nuance and variety of our award-winning sake.
Location:
Please arrive promptly in our taproom to start your tour. The location is:
34 34th Street, Inner courtyard
Industry City Building 6
Brooklyn, NY 11232
events.vendor: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting

events.title: Sake Server Certification
events.description:
This is a one day in person seminar from 10am to 5pm with a 1 hour lunch break.
Level up your service skills in sake! This one day sake training certification course will teach you all the ins and outs of proper service and sales for sake in your your restaurant or retail shop. There is no prerequisite and this course is open to anyone wishing to get training in professional service of sake. The course consists of four modules.
1) Introduction to sake & Brewery Tour
2) sake sales techniques
3) sake service and presentation
4) sake menus and pairing
After the one day training course in completed, you’ll have to option to complete an online course review and online exam within two weeks time. Upon successful completion of the review and exam, you’ll receive the Sake Studies Center “Sake Server Certification” diploma.
As a graduate of our professional Sake Server Certification, you’ll also have ongoing access to our free online sake community for the sake industry. Ask questions, and stay in touch with your fellow sake students and industry leaders.
events.vendor: Sake Server Certification

events.title: July 14 Semifinals World Cup Watch Party
events.description:
Get tickets here: https://wl.eventim.us/event/july-14-semifinals-world-cup-watch-party/697369?afflky=Getitrightthefirsttime
Come to Brooklyn’s Best World Cup Watch Party Experience!
Come watch the competition on our 15-foot wide projection screen and listen to the game in full concert sound under the stars in Sunset Park, Brooklyn.
Scheduled matches:
3:00pm Semifinals TBD
This event requires a $5 RSVP, which includes a $6 discount on your first order at our venue bar.
There will be drink specials, but don’t forget to take advantage of the 2-for-1 drink ticket when you check out! Space is limited and RSVP doesn’t guarantee entry. Buying advanced drink tickets guarantees entry!
NO STROLLERS or children under the age of 3.
Limited seating (about 100 seats), tons of standing room. SO THAT WE MAY INCLUDE AS MANY PEOPLE AS POSSIBLE AND FOR YOUR SAFETY, PLEASE DO NOT BRING YOUR OWN CHAIRS. Trust us, the second the game starts everybody is up and cheering anyway!
While we do our best to gauge the number of RSVPs, entry is still on a first-come, first-served basis, so please arrive early! Once we hit capacity, we won’t be able to let anyone else in.
No outside beverages. There will be drink specials all day long from our courtyard bar. Feel free to bring in food from the adjoining food hall!
Event Info
Date: July 14, 2026
Doors: 2:00pm
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device.
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can get affordable drinks from the venue’s bar and order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.
events.vendor: July 14 Semifinals World Cup Watch Party

events.title: Zine Club
events.description:
Join us every two weeks for Zine Club, a gathering dedicated to DIY bookmaking, self-publishing, creative experimentation, and community. Whether you’re new to zines or working on an ongoing project, this is a space to explore ideas, share work, and be creative in community with other artists, writers, activists, and creatives of all kinds.
Each begins with a quick demo of a technique or process you might incorporate in your own work. Explore a wide range of materials and techniques available in our studio, including collage, drawing, image transfer, stenciling, and printmaking.
Zine Club is led by Lista Studios instructor Wil Bigio, a graphic designer, illustrator, and dedicated doodler based in New York.
Details:
Meets every other Wednesday (first and third of the month) from 6:00 pm – 8:30 pm
Basic materials provided (paper, collage supplies, and printmaking tools)
events.vendor: Zine Club

events.title: Plant Adoption X Pot DIY
events.description:
What happens to your daily coffee or bubble tea cups after the last sip?
💡 DIY your own plant pot using recycled plastic cups and take a plant home! No experience required—just bring your imagination.
Highlights: Free admission and all supplies included.
🥤 Cup & Bring-Your-Own Policy:
We will provide pre-collected, clean plastic cups at the venue. However, we highly encourage you to bring one or two washed, empty plastic cups from your favorite local café to give them a second life!
👋 Meet Your Organizer:
This workshop is hosted by Jessy, an illustrator and product designer based in NYC. Instagram 👉 https://www.instagram.com/jessyyy_liu/
Plant Adoption x Pot DIY is sponsored, in part, by the Greater New York Arts Development Fund of the New York City Department of Cultural Affairs, administered by Brooklyn Arts Council. The workshop is hosted at Brooklyn Creative Reuse 254 36th Street, Space C244, 2nd Floor.
events.vendor: Plant Adoption X Pot DIY

events.title: Brotaku: Tekken 8 Tournament Comedy Show
events.description:
Stand-up comedians roast players while they compete live in a chaotic Tekken tournament.
16 competitors enter. One leaves with $250. Everyone else gets cooked.
Expect:
• stand-up to open the show
• player intros + live roasting
• wildcard players pulled from the audience
• unhinged energy the entire time
Part tournament, part comedy show, part public execution.
Like playing video games on your friend’s couch—if your friends were ruthless comedians.

Details:
• Thursday, July 16 PM
• Doors 7:00 PM | Show 8:00 PM
• Eastville Comedy Club (Brooklyn)
• 2 drink minimum

Tickets:
• Competitor Pass ($27): guaranteed tournament spot
• Audience ($19): watch + chance to be pulled as a wildcard
Competitor slots fill fast. Last show sold out.
All tickets include 2 takoyaki balls + 1 meat skewer from Karl’s Balls and Izakaya Juraku.
events.vendor: Brotaku: Tekken 8 Tournament Comedy Show

events.title: Chris "Catman" Grant Presents: Si, Pero…No Live
events.description:
Chris Grant presents: Si, Pero…No Live

Chris Grant brings his signature high-energy, culturally sharp comedy to Eastville for a special late-night experience.

Blending real-life storytelling, Dominican culture, and unapologetic humor, ‘Si, Pero…No Live’ delivers a fast-paced lineup of top NYC comedians in an intimate room built for big laughs.

This is not a drop-in show it’s a curated comedy experience with strong performances, real crowd interaction, and a vibe you feel the second you walk in.

Come ready to laugh, react, and be part of the night.
